Johns Hopkins Medicine and Cleveland Clinic: Normal SpO2 is 95-100%, but hospitals maintain SpO2 above 92%

pi_items.verified_value: ICU alarm fatigue audit - well-documented patient safety issue
pi_items.source: Joint Commission has designated alarm management as a National Patient Safety Goal since 2014. Between 2009-2012, 98 sentinel events with 80 deaths reported. ICUs experience 85-99% unnecessary alarms with up to 350 alarms per bed per day.
Confirmed in README.md line 96 and verifier.py line 26: Pass threshold is 60 points
Confirmed in setup_task.sh line 25 and verifier.py line 27: Report must be saved to /home/ga/Desktop/alarm_audit.txt
setup_openice.sh and README indicate data comes from OpenICE device adapters and simulators, not real patient data

privileged_info_summary: All vital sign parameters in the task metadata are verified against authoritative medical sources. Heart rate normal range (60-100 bpm), respiratory rate (12-20 breaths/min), and SpO2 threshold (≥92%) match standard ICU clinical guidelines from Johns Hopkins, Cleveland Clinic, and NCBI medical literature. Alarm fatigue is confirmed as a Joint Commission National Patient Safety Goal with significant historical impact (80+ deaths from 98 sentinel events between 2009-2012). The task uses OpenICE, an open-source medical device interoperability platform developed by the MD PnP Program at MGH Harvard, which generates synthetic simulated patient data. The required deliverable is a plain-text alarm audit report to /home/ga/Desktop/alarm_audit.txt with passing score of 60 points.
